question_text: She ___ to school every day.
They ___ playing football right now.
I ___ my homework yesterday.
He ___ a doctor when he grows up.
Look! The bird ___ in the sky.
Write the past tense form of the verb 'run'.
Fill in the blank: She ___ (eat) breakfast at 8 AM every morning.
Identify the tense in this sentence: They are watching a movie.
Explain the Simple Present Tense. State its uses and write five example sentences of your own.
What is the Present Continuous Tense? Explain when it is used and give four examples showing different uses.
By the time he arrived she ___ already left.
___ you ever visited London?
She ___ for three hours when the power went out.
The report ___ by the manager before the meeting started.
By next year I ___ in this company for a decade.
Rewrite this sentence in the Past Perfect Tense: She eats an apple.
Identify and name the tense: The students have been waiting for the teacher since 9 AM.
Correct the tense error in this sentence: Yesterday, she has gone to the market.
correct_answer: Yesterday, she went to the market.

question_text: Compare the Present Perfect Tense and the Simple Past Tense. Explain the key differences and provide three example sentences for each.
Explain the Past Continuous Tense and the Past Perfect Continuous Tense. Write two sentences of your own to illustrate each tense.
Which sentence is grammatically correct?
option_a: She has went to the market.
option_b: She had went to the market.
option_c: She has gone to the market.
option_d: She have gone to the market.

question_text: Choose the sentence that correctly uses the Future Perfect Continuous Tense.
option_a: She will been working for six hours.
option_b: She will have been working for six hours.
option_c: She would been working for six hours.
option_d: She has been working for six hours.

question_text: The passive voice of 'They will have completed the project' is ___.
option_a: The project will be completed by them.
option_b: The project will have been completed by them.
option_c: The project is completed by them.
option_d: The project has been completed by them.

question_text: Identify the error: 'He is knowing the answer since childhood.'
She ___ the novel three times before the exam, so she was well prepared.
Transform this sentence into the Future Perfect Tense: She submits the report.
correct_answer: She will have submitted the report.

question_text: Explain the difference between 'I have lived here for five years' and 'I lived here for five years'. When would you use each?
correct_answer: Present Perfect indicates still living there; Simple Past indicates no longer living there.

question_text: Rewrite using the correct tense: 'When I reached the station, the train already left.'
correct_answer: When I reached the station, the train had already left.

question_text: Discuss all twelve tenses in English. For each tense, state its form, its primary use, and give one original example sentence.

Analyse the use of tenses in the following paragraph and explain why each tense was chosen by the writer: 'By the time the detectives arrived, the thief had vanished. He had been hiding in the building for weeks, but he knew they were closing in.'
